programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Crystal Palace v Manchester City at Selhurst Park and Chelsea v Newcastle United at Stamford Bridge. The Eagles had a woeful recent record against the champions, having lost eight of their previous 10 meetings in all competitions. However, Roy Hodgson's men did pull off a shock away victory over the Citizens in December, winning 3-2 at the Etihad Stadium. Plus, action from Aston Villa v Brighton & Hove Albion at Villa Park, Bournemouth v Norwich City at the Vitality Stadium, Everton v West Ham United at Goodison Park, Leicester City v Burnley at the King Power Stadium, Tottenham Hotspur v Watford at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium, and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Southampton at Molineux(n)

programme.title: Countryfile

Margherita Taylor meets the brass band making a 'big noise' in the area. She also explores the extraordinary Puzzlewood to discover how a landscape like this is protected. Adam Henson is on his farm keeping everything crossed as Lexie, his Suffolk Punch mare, gets a pregnancy scan(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Battle Abbey in East Sussex, where treasures include a diamond ring that was purchased for £1, a 17th-century violin and a unique telegram from Adolf Hitler. A set of iconic weather signs and a valuable princess's dress are among the other items turning up on the day(n)

programme.title: World on Fire
programme.desc: Harry and his unit are fighting for their lives in the town of Louvain, Belgium, where the German forces far outnumber those of the Allies. Grzegorz and Konrad are woken by a German troop, while back in Warsaw Kasia and Tomasz's resistance activity has horrific consequences. Webster is shocked to learn more about the rise of anti-Semitism in Paris when a Jewish couple come seeking his help(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Manchester United v Liverpool. Mark Chapman presents action from the day's only Premier League fixture, as the fierce rivals met at Old Trafford. Liverpool had not won here since the 2013/14, but they will have gone into the match with confidence after winning their first eight league games this season, while United had endured a disappointing start to the campaign(n)

programme.title: Football Going Vegan: Jermaine Jenas Investigates
programme.desc: The former player explores the rise of veganism in football, a somewhat controversial subject. He talks to celebrity nutritionist Rhiannon Lambert, cooks a couple of dishes with Instagram chef Miguel Barclay, while also spending the day at the first ever vegan football club, Forest Green Rovers. Along his journey, Jermaine sees if he can incorporate a vegan lifestyle into his family life and goes head to head with vegan activist Ed Winters, as well as catching up with vegan footballer Chris Smalling(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic gold medallists in cycling and boxing respectively Joanna Rowsell Shand and Luke Campbell, former Great Britain sprinter John Regis and Scotland rugby union star Tommy Seymour join team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: The secret history of thousands of second-hand cars and the highly unusual treatment one customer received from an exclusive dating agency(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits properties in Preston and Liverpool in need of renovation, and Dion Dublin is in Nottingham to meet a couple with differing views on their project(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Secret filming is the undoing of an opportunist attempting to exaggerate his personal injury claim, and insurers deal with a rear-end shunt that has plenty of twists and turns(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Two shop owners are subjected to a torrent of racist abuse by drunken men, and a pub landlord's son rushes into the premises when the burglar alarm goes off(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Sid and Imogen have a heart to heart, while the staff at The Mill struggle to deal with the closure of the Icon Bar(n)

programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the quiz in which a physics student, a facilities manager and a customer advisor must choose between working together to pool their knowledge or going it alone(n)

programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin puts Melton Mowbray and its property market to the test for his buyers, who believe the Leicestershire town is ideal for them to spend £325,000 on their first home(n)

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: Inside Out London
programme.desc: Louise Hulland reveals how councils are cracking down on illegal Airbnb lets, and Jon Gibson investigates what is being done to stop kids getting into danger on railway tracks(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The highs and lows of life with the residents of Albert Square, Walford(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: He was the rock star of investing. Hundreds of thousands of people trusted him with their savings and pensions. But Neil Woodford's funds lost billions and now investors are waiting to find out how much of their money they will get back. So how can you know who to trust with your cash? Richard Bilton investigates the secretive world of fund managers - the people who gamble with your money(n)

programme.title: Dublin Murders
programme.desc: As Katy is finally laid to rest, Rob continues to be haunted by memories of the past and Jonathan Devlin and his friends' disturbing presence in the woods on the fateful day Peter and Jamie disappeared. The public and media pressure to find Katy's killer is intense, and Superintendent O'Kelly is concerned that all lines of inquiry have led to naught. Rob, however, is increasingly certain that his memories are leading him to the truth. Cassie receives a frantic dawn phone call from Frank, who is in Wicklow following the trail of corrupt land deals along the path of the planned motorway(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Mairead's much-loved fridge finally gives up on them. Remembering the item was originally bought in a town many miles away, Mairead decides that a family trip to that same place to buy a replacement will be just the thing to bond with her son. After some discussion Jock is eventually included, bringing along his keen navigation skills and unique sense of entertainment to pass the time. During the journey, all the travellers learn lessons about family connections. Comedy, starring Alex Murphy and Chris Walley(n)

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, as guests including comedian Ross Noble joining regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Original Terminator stars Arnold Schwarzenegger and Linda Hamilton join the host to discuss their being reunited in the movie's latest instalment, Terminator: Dark Fate. Plus, iconic lead singer of Blondie, Debbie Harry talks about her autobiography, Face It, and Nadiya Hussain discusses her frank and honest new memoir, Finding My Voice. With music by DJ Spoony's Garage Classical featuring the Sugababes(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Ways that consumers could be caught out by a catch in terms and conditions, and a couple's battle over whether their home is fit to be called habitable(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a rundown bungalow in Berkshire, a Victorian terrace in Norfolk and a pair of semis with a river view in Burton-on-Trent(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Anonymous tip-offs reveal an outrageous plan to cash in after a flood ruins a house and, when a missing watch turns up, it is not the good news that the claimant expected(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood presents the stories of a student who intervened to stop a thief getting away and a crook who stole a large TV, but only brought a small getaway car(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents from Leominster, Herefordshire, as Paul Laidlaw and Mark Ashley help the teams raise the most money. Natasha also shows off her favourite ceramic frog(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al returns to some shocking news, while Becky meets Izzie for the first time. Emma helps the daughter of a terminally ill patient to come to terms with the situation(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the quiz in which two students and a retired GP must choose between working together to pool their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman visits the seaside town of Ayr to help buyers with £400,000 to spend, who have pinpointed Ayr as a potential location due to its close proximity to the beach and city(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team are in Bradford, where they restore an old lawnmower and an antique cabinet, and find a creative new use for a cable drum(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Two antiques experts are pitted against each other as they travel across the UK in search of bargains before heading to an auction to find out whose haul proves most popular with buyers(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The highs and lows of life with the residents of Albert Square, Walford(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric agrees to undergo the dangerous brain surgery proposed by neurosurgeon Max. During the procedure, Ric has visions of his dead son and lost love, who present him with a terrible choice between life and death. A pregnant Helen returns to Holby, and Dom tries to support Lofty as he faces the prospect of fatherhood(n)

programme.title: Dublin Murders
programme.desc: Frank comes up with a plan for Cassie to go undercover as her double to discover who killed her, but she refuses to be involved. However, a savage rejection from Rob that leaves her feeling betrayed leads her to have a change of heart. As the investigation into Katy drags to a halt, Rob becomes fixated on tracking down two of Jonathan's old friends, convinced they are the key to solving both mysteries(n)

programme.title: Jesy Nelson: 'Odd One Out'
programme.desc: When Jesy Nelson rose to fame with pop band Little Mix, she was abused online for being 'the fat one'. In this personal documentary, the singer opens up about the impact cyberbullies had on her life and the effect on her mental health. Her fellow Little Mix members Perrie, Leigh-Anne and Jade discuss how Jesy's torment affected the ban, while her boyfriend, Love Island star Chris Hughes, recalls falling in love with someone who was clearly damaged by the barrage of negativity she experienced from social media trolls(n)

programme.title: Stupid Man, Smart Phone
programme.desc: Russell Kane is joined by internet celebrity Mazzi Maz, and together they are pursued across Poland by guard dogs, with only their phones to help them avoid capture. Contacting a teenager preparing for the apocalypse proves useful in increasing their survival skills, but have a harder time finding online tips on driving a tank, hotwiring a motorbike or skinning a rabbit(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Life in a Cottage Garden with Carol Klein
programme.desc: In September and October, the horticulturist harvests seeds and pricks out young plants to aid growth. She also prepares the garden for visitors to her Open Garden Scheme day, and enlists her daughters' help to decide the future of a cankered apple tree(n)

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Weather forecaster Carol Kirkwood takes a journey through the world of horticulture, receiving inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters. The series progresses in alphabetical order, and this programme features themes beginning with the letter A, and includes archive material from Gardeners' World, Countryfile and The Gardener's Year(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don plants out berried shrubs to help feed wildlife in the winter, harvests pumpkins and shows how to make use of fallen autumnal leaves. Carol Klein shows the planting partners still making an impact in the borders of the Bishop's Palace and Gardens in Somerset, while Adam Frost is in the Cotswolds finding out about some surprising planting schemes designed with animals in mind. Frances Tophill summarises her first season on her allotment in Kent and Rachel de Thame updates her containers to ensure that they give interest for the upcoming seasons ahead(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team explores Wembury in Devon, with Anita Rani learning about its naval history, Joe Crowley investigating its rock pools as he takes part in an annual nature survey and Ellie Harrison going in search of its marine wildlife. Elsewhere, Adam Henson is moving some of his sheep and Tom Heap is on the trail of the ramraiding gangs ripping out the ATMs on which villages rely(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's Asian Odyssey
programme.desc: Part one of two. An analysis of her DNA leads Nadiya Hussain to a surprising discovery that sees her travel to far-flung places to explore the people, places and food there. The former Great British Bake Off winner begins by visiting Cambodia, where she uncovers a bakery school training up girls from tough backgrounds in the art of French patisserie, before heading to Thailand, where she learns how to make a curry paste unique to the Muslim community on the tiny island of Koh Sukhorn(n)

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo(n)

programme.title: Live PDC Darts Champions League
programme.desc: Coverage of the concluding group-stage matches from the Morningside Arena in Leicester, as the line-up for the semi-finals is confirmed. Gary Anderson needed to defeat top seed Michael van Gerwen to reach the last four in 2018 and eventually prevailed 10-9 against the Dutchman, who joined him in the semi-finals, while Mensur Suljovic and Peter Wright progressed from the other group(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: The RNLI volunteers at Lough Ree Lifeboat Station race to the rescue of the last inhabitant of a remote island. On the north-east coast of England, the Blyth crew races to find a kayaker in trouble in the North Sea, while at Tower station in Central London, a dog is trapped by the tide on the River Thames(n)

programme.title: Africa with Ade Adepitan
programme.desc: The presenter explores central Africa, travelling through Gabon, the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda. He investigates plans to develop a sustainable version of palm oil farming, meets a champion boxer helping to rehabilitate child soldiers, and talks to one of Africa's most outspoken political campaigners. He also reports on how conflict between militia groups influenced by foreign powers lies at the heart of the problems that have made the Democratic Republic of Congo arguably the most chaotic and corrupt country in Africa, as well as discovering how the conflict has impacted on the country's few remaining gorillas(n)

programme.title: Live PDC Darts Champions League
programme.desc: Live coverage of the semi-finals and final from Morningside Arena in Leicester, as the fourth staging of the competition featuring the world's top eight players concludes. Peter Wright and Gary Anderson ensured last year's final was an all-Scottish affair when they overcame Dutchman Michael van Gerwen and Austria's Mensur Suljovic respectively in their last-four matches. It was then Anderson who comfortably prevailed 11-4 in the showpiece match to add this title to a glittering list of honours that includes two World Championships and two Premier Leagues, as well as the UK Open, World Matchplay and Player Championship Finals(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Katherine Ryan comperes an evening of stand-up at London's Hammersmith Apollo, treating the audience to her own brand of humour and introducing routines by comedy circuit stars Henning Wehn and James Acaster(n)

programme.title: Love in the Countryside
programme.desc: Sara Cox heads to Somerset to help give equine dentist Victoria a leg up the love ladder. Victoria adores her country life, but the 35-year-old's dating pool is small and when it comes to men she is incredibly picky. Sara encourages Victoria to be more open-minded and she speed-dates five gents, before inviting her favourite three back to Somerset to sample rural life. Meanwhile, 24-year-old vet George is doing his best to woo the three ladies he's entertaining at his home in Hay-on-Wye, on the Welsh border(n)

programme.title: 99 Homes (2014)
programme.desc: A single father desperately trying to support his son and his mother goes to work for the ruthless businessman who evicted them from their home. He is seduced by the glamorous world of high finance and seeks the chance to provide for his family's security - but to get it, he will have to deprive other families of their homes. Drama, starring Andrew Garfield, Michael Shannon and Laura Dern(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Leicester, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three semi-detached homes in York - including a two-up, two-down 1930s house reinvented for the modern market and a 1970s communal building - are in the spotlight(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Frankie returns to Holby, keen to take Isla back. Essie is not convinced she is truly ready for the demands of parenthood, and tries to persuade her to leave her daughter behind. A top neurosurgeon has a radical idea for how to treat Ric's tumour, but Serena is not convinced by the plan. Ric is left to consider both their positions, and decide what he wants to do. Jac returns from holiday, and asserts her authority over an overworked Kian(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: EJ Osborne use his salvaging expertise at Llanfoist Recycling Centre in Monmouthshire, searching for three items that he can rescue and revamp. A collection of trampoline poles are worked on by carpenter Ollie Allen, while lighting specialist Guy Trench has to come up with a bright idea for a paraffin can and a pair of blow torches. For his own project, EJ has a large desk to keep him busy(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Sonali Shah explores Arundel in West Sussex, in her quest for the ideal location as her buyers seek the right place to spend their £900,000 on. She hands over the keys to some surprising houses, but will her self-confessed 'instant buyers' decide to seal the deal on any of them?(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: The RNLI volunteers at Lough Ree Lifeboat Station race to the rescue of the last inhabitant of a remote island. programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament(n)

programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: Probate detectives discover a surprise connection to gun-making in Birmingham, while searching for the beneficiaries to a £100,000 estate. The case of a west London man with Indian ancestry, proves a major challenge for the team(n)

programme.title: Yes Chef
programme.desc: Return of the culinary challenge hosted by Sheree Murphy in which leading chefs choose their perfect partner from three home cooks, beginning with Nigel Haworth(n)

programme.title: Hidden Kingdoms
programme.desc: Documentary revealing the survival techniques used by the animal kingdom's smallest creatures. North America's Sonoran Desert is home to the grasshopper mouse, which is capable of disarming scorpions and centipedes twice its size, and displays a power and athleticism that enables it to avoid the attentions of rattlesnakes and tarantulas. In Africa, the elephant shrew, known as the sengi, employs speed to keep its distance from predators, following a meticulously constructed - and maintained - network of tiny racetracks in the savannah. The film concludes with Hidden Kingdoms Revealed, a 10-minute report on how the documentary was made. Narrated by Stephen Fry(n)

programme.title: The Hunt
programme.desc: The challenges predators face as they stalk their prey, and revealing how it is far more common for hunts to end in failure. The first episode demonstrates how leopards use stealth and concealment to get close to their prey, while African wild dogs have to work as a team. The programme also features footage of killer whales, which rely on their intelligence, Nile crocodiles that can wait years between meals, spiders able to spin 25 foot webs and amur falcons, which travel 16,000 miles every year to hunt insects. Narrated by David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin shows a cattery owner and a handyman from Clevedon the tricks of the trade for buying and selling collectibles, before an auction showdown(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Andi Oliver, Ivo Graham, Phill Jupitus and Kate Humble test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week, with a winner declared each Friday(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal is joined by the latest couple to be eliminated from the competition after losing the dance-off at the weekend. Plus, interviews with the remaining pairs(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents coverage of the draw, as the non-league clubs to have progressed to the first-round proper enter alongside the teams from League One and League Two(n)

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Paul Murton begins an exploration of the nation's lochs by travelling through the wilds surrounding Loch Etive. He witnesses the spectacular tidal race at the Falls of Lora, where kayakers ride a three-metre standing wave, and goes high-altitude camping on a hill opposite Buchaille Etive More, watching the sunset light up the hundreds of lochans across Rannoch Moor(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another first-round match as transit fans the Journeymen take on cheese-lovers the Turophiles. The teams must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of charge and release, band and break up, locate and move out of position, and courage and deter(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The academic quiz's penultimate first-round match takes place, with four students from Imperial College, London pitting their wits against a team from Brasenose College, Oxford in a bid to reach the second stage of the competition. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: The Day California Burned
programme.desc: Documentary examining the devastating wildfire that swept through northern California in November 2018, causing the deaths of more than 80 people and approximately $16.5billion of damage. The film features interviews with firefighters, police officers and local residents who came face to face with the fire, including a nurse who evacuated her patients before finding herself trapped in the middle of the blaze(n)

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Julia finds that working from home is impossible due to all the tasks she has to do for her family. Meg tells her this is her 'Mother's Load' - all the things that no-one else will do, but need to be done to keep the home running. Julia removes herself to the cafe to work, but even there finds it's not that easy to get motivated. A possible solution presents itself when she shares a table with a fellow freelancer, but is Julia taking advantage of him? Meanwhile, much to her surprise, Liz finds herself bored to tears when her youngest starts nursery and she is alone during the day, and Kevin has problems when he takes on responsibility for a school fund-raising project(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Miriam's Dead Good Adventure
programme.desc: Part one of two. Miriam Margolyes explores mortality, visiting a home for dementia patients and meeting a scientist in the US who claims that technology will soon provide a cure for the ageing process. She attends a festival in California for people determined to prolong their lives by any means, and discovers that laughter may help people to live longer and stay healthier(n)

programme.title: Miriam's Dead Good Adventure
programme.desc: Part two of two. Miriam Margolyes questions if it is ever possible to be ready for death, helping a terminal cancer patient to plan her wake, and building her own personalised flatpack coffin with a support group for people facing death. She also meets a doctor who uses tranquilisers to treat terminal patients' anxiety and a rapper with bowel and liver cancer who refuses to give up on life. Plus, a representative of the right to die movement explains why he believes all rational people over 50 have the right to end their lives, and reveals his invention to make this easier(n)

programme.title: Nomad: In the Footsteps of Bruce Chatwin
programme.desc: Documentary in which Werner Herzog sets out on a journey inspired by his friend, writer and adventurer Bruce Chatwin, who had a passion for the nomadic life. Carrying the rucksack that Chatwin gave him on his deathbed, Herzog travels to South America, Australia and the Welsh borders, uncovering stories of lost tribes, wanderers and dreamers(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team explores Wembury in Devon, with Anita Rani learning about its naval history, Joe Crowley investigating its rock pools as he takes part in an annual nature survey and Ellie Harrison going in search of its marine wildlife. Elsewhere, Adam Henson is moving some of his sheep and Tom Heap is on the trail of the ramraiding gangs ripping out the ATMs on which villages rely(n)

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is in Surrey checking out the cast-offs at Witley Recycling Centre, which this time are an old chest of drawers, a pair of chairs and a woollen blanket(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin puts Melton Mowbray and its property market to the test for his buyers, who believe the Leicestershire town is ideal for them to spend £325,000 on their first home(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: The third leg of Dave Myers and Si King's trip sees them on the trail of Oklahoma's famous beef. In Oklahoma City, a classic T-bone steak is followed by a visit to the city's Vietnamese community, where they knock up their national dish pho - a broth made of beef or chicken. After checking out El Reno's unique onion burger the duo hook up with a couple of fire crews who like to cook - especially brisket. An organic sweet potato farm inspires Dave and Si to make sweet potato brownies, before they finish this stretch on a cattle ranch with America's most iconic custodian of the land - the cowboy(n)

programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Peter Andre presents an appeal on behalf of the Diana Award(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond, presented by Jo Coburn(n)

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: There is a twist in the tale as the detectives search for relatives of a south London woman, while another case uncovers the story of a man who led a double life(n)

programme.title: Yes Chef
programme.desc: Culinary challenge hosted by Sheree Murphy in which top chef Amandine Chaignot puts three home cooks through a series of challenges, including how to prepare a squab(n)

programme.title: Hidden Kingdoms
programme.desc: Stephen Fry narrates another documentary revealing the survival techniques of the animal kingdom's smallest creatures. This film follows a young tree shrew as it darts through the rainforest of Borneo on the lookout for food. The creature's super-fast metabolism means it is always hungry, but as it searches for fruit, it must avoid fire ants, giant snakes and even a carnivorous plant. Cameras also follow a male chipmunk collecting acorns in preparation for winter - but while he's out, a bigger, more aggressive neighbour ransacks his store. Can he get the nuts back?(n)

programme.title: The Hunt
programme.desc: Cameras follow predators living in the Arctic, who face a particularly challenging search for food, as the environment around them constantly changes with the seasons, from a continent of ice in perpetual darkness to a scattering of islands in constant sunshine. A polar bear climbs to reach a birds' nest, while another makes holes in the ice to catch seals. Plus, a look at how Arctic foxes have adapted to catch auks in mid-flight and the hunting strategies of Arctic wolves. Narrated by David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin shows a businessman and a reprographic manager from Norfolk are shown the tricks of the trade for buying and selling collectibles, before an auction showdown(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Andi Oliver, Ivo Graham, Phill Jupitus and Kate Humble test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week, with a winner declared each Friday(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal chats to the couple who avoided elimination in Sunday night's dance-off. Plus, a look ahead to the music featured in the next show and more exclusive interviews(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Daniel Brocklebank and Hayley Tamaddon search for antiques in East Anglia, taking a trip to Great Yarmouth's Pleasure Beach and discovering a scheme to rehouse unwanted dogs(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: RNLI volunteers at Calshot on the south coast race to the rescue of a 180-metre-long cargo ship which has run aground on a sandbank, and is now listing at a dangerous angle. At the most northerly lifeboat station on the British mainland, the crew gets called out to investigate a personal locator beacon which has been set off on a remote stretch of rocky coastline, and in Kinsale, volunteers come to the aid of a horse(n)

programme.title: Love in the Countryside
programme.desc: Sara Cox helps Welsh shepherd Ioan get a leg up the love ladder, but when he invites three ladies back home, his demanding work threatens to obstruct his shot at romance. Meanwhile, vet George and equine dentist Victoria are both in the grips of a dating conundrum, sheepdog trainer Katy leaves the remote Cumbrian fells to see if romance will blossom with ex-motorbike champ David, and Sara finds out if any of the rural romantics believe they have found everlasting love in the countryside(n)

programme.title: Defending the Guilty
programme.desc: The pupils must address a court for the first time, which for Will means the seemingly straightforward case of defending a drug addict failed by an uncaring system. However, his confidence is rocked by the breakdown of his relationship, and he questions if he is really cut out for a career in law. Caroline is asked to give a lecture to the female bar association, but they are less than pleased by what she has to say. Liam and Danielle have contrasting first days in court, and Ashley has a bad day all round(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Babies: Their Wonderful World
programme.desc: Paediatrician Dr Guddi Singh presents a documentary exploring the crucial changes of the first two years of a child's life, bringing together babies and scientists from around the world to take part in one of the most ambitious scientific studies about infants ever attempted. The first edition follows an experiment that looks at temperament, how tablet use affects babies, what toddlers understand about gender, and where biases come from(n)

programme.title: A Timewatch Guide
programme.desc: Vanessa Collingridge explores how the public's view of ancient Egyptian queen Cleopatra has changed over the years, from Roman propaganda through to Shakespeare's portrayal, and in the golden age of Hollywood, where she became a doomed romantic heroine. The presenter seeks the views of academic experts and refers to archive BBC documentaries and drama to explore Cleopatra's personal life and role as a politician, charting how throughout history the leader's image has been subject to myths and cliches(n)

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: The crew of HMS Queen Elizabeth completes the vessel's sea trials, testing an innovative new firefighting system for the first time and marking the 100th anniversary of a plane being successfully landed on a moving ship. The carrier returns to Portsmouth, which will be its home port for the next 50 years, while the pilots of its fighter jets go to the US for additional training(n)

programme.title: Destination Titan
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of a British team involved in preparing the European Space Agency's Huygens probe for its two-billion-mile journey to Saturn's largest moon, a voyage that took seven years. The film explores the sacrifices involved in the project, and the scientists talk about their hopes for the probe, and thoughts on its successful landing in 2005(n)

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Generation
programme.desc: Stories of courage, suffering and survival from servicemen and women between 1942 and 1945, as revealed by a generation of people now in their nineties and hundreds. This instalment includes the story of Fergus Anckorn who was once the youngest member of the Magic Circle and - at 96 - is now the oldest. He was taken prisoner by the Japanese in Singapore and put to work on the Burma Railway. He managed to survive using his magic to entertain his captors, who rewarded him with food that kept him and his fellow prisoners alive(n)

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Generation
programme.desc: A look at individuals who played their part in creating a more culturally diverse post-war Britain, including pioneering actor Earl Cameron, who broke through the colour bar in the film industry. Other well-known names also considered are gay rights activist George Montague, and Brian Rix, who has campaigned passionately on the issue of disability. Plus, Arctic Convoy survivor Austin Byrne and former prisoner of war Jim Purcell share their stories and memories of the era(n)

programme.title: The Sinner
programme.desc: With Ambrose's help, Cora tries to recover missing memories, which lead the detective to a new suspect. Meanwhile, Mason attempts to find answers on his own and suspects JD of involvement in his wife's predicament(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Arthur leads the Peaky Blinders in their plot to take over London's Eden Club, and an angry Sabini manages to convince Alfie to put their feud to one side and help him destroy the gang. Tommy hires society darling May Carleton to train his race horse and they quickly form a close relationship, and he later discovers the truth about the mission he is being forced to undertake for Major Campbell. Is he on the verge of losing everything as he becomes embroiled in Campbell's political game? Back in Birmingham, Polly's son Michael soon discovers the dark side of the family business(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Tommy's power base in London is obliterated and Arthur and Michael are imprisoned as Major Campbell makes his influence felt. Distraught at the prospect of losing her son to a lengthy spell in prison, Polly compromises herself to secure Michael's freedom. However, her actions risk damaging already fragile relationships. While attempting to regain the upper hand, Tommy is paid a visit by someone from his past. Old wounds are reopened and he finds himself having to make an impossible decision. Starring Cillian Murphy and Sam Neill(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: It is the day of the Epsom Derby and Tommy is there to carry out the mission given to him by Major Campbell, but he plans to turn the situation to his advantage by bringing his gang to the racecourse and striking a blow against Sabini. However, more than one of his adversaries have forged secret schemes, and as he readies his troops, Tommy receives shocking news that will change everything. Period gangster drama, starring Cillian Murphy and Sam Neill(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Pubs, Ponds and Power: The Story of the Village
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson explores the history of the fishing village of Port Isaac in Cornwall, discovering a place where people exploited a range of natural resources to make a living and find profits far beyond Britain's shores(n)

programme.title: American History's Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian reveals the myths and deceptions told following the United States' emergence as a superpower after the Second World War. The 1950s and early 1960s in America is remembered as a golden era of abundance, harmony and the American dream made real. Lucy Worsley reveals that to have been a carefully constructed illusion and that the era of America's supremacy was a time of government deception, racial conflict and fears of nuclear annihilation(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: The story of one of the world's biggest white collar-heists, which saw a Malaysian wealth fund looted of $3.5billion and involved government corruption at the highest level, abuse of power and international money laundering. With little to go on, investigative reporters from the New York Times, Wall Street Journal and Hollywood Reporter retrace the dirty money, via real-estate deals and movie financing including The Wolf of Wall Street, back to the top echelons of the Malaysian government. The Prime Minister and his inner circle are implicated, assets are frozen, money is seized and the Malaysian people fight back(n)

programme.title: The Machine Gun and Skye's Band of Brothers
programme.desc: Neil Oliver examines the development of the Maxim machine gun - which was capable of firing 666 rounds a minute - and explores the devastating impact of its use on the battlefields of France by the German army in the First World War. He follows the stories of soldiers from Skye who went to the front together as part of the Cameron Highlanders, drawing on letters sent home and the details of the battles they fought to portray how the machine gun attacks left a gaping hole in a tightly knit community(n)

programme.title: The Story of China
programme.desc: Michael Wood tells the story of the Ming dynasty, founded by peasant rebel Zhu Yuanzhang, who fought his way up to become the Hongwu emperor. The film explores the importance of architecture to the Ming, visiting the emperor's capital of Nanjing, with its 21 miles of massive walls. Following the trail, Michael goes to the Bao family village and sees the locals act a Ming murder story, before hearing about voyages to Africa and the Gulf decades before Columbus, and learning about the silk industry, ceramics and lacquer-making(n)

programme.title: Pubs, Ponds and Power: The Story of the Village
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson explores the history of the fishing village of Port Isaac in Cornwall, discovering a place where people exploited a range of natural resources to make a living and find profits far beyond Britain's shores(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Thomas Shelby's long-awaited day has arrived, but in the middle of the celebrations, a mysterious visitor puts the entire Shelby family in danger, pulling the mob boss into a web of intrigue more lethal than anything he has yet encountered. Period crime drama, starring Cillian Murphy(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: On the orders of the secretive Economic League, Tommy goes to London to meet the wildly eccentric and volatile Duke Leon - and when he is given his mission, he soon realises the extreme lengths his new paymasters are willing to go to in their quest for power. Back in Birmingham, John's temper gets the better of him, and a personal dispute with the Italians threatens to explode into a devastating war. Tommy struggles to regain control of the situation - only to discover how extensive his new enemy's hold on the Peaky Blinders is. Cillian Murphy, Helen McCrory and Joe Cole star, with an appearance by Paddy Considine(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Tommy responds to the Italians' actions, setting him on a path of deadly vengeance that threatens to take him to his darkest place yet - and could also splinter the family. And when he meets Grand Duchess Izabella, the real force behind his mission, he discovers there's a traitor in his paymasters' midst - one who could unravel the entire plan. Meanwhile, Polly realises the depths of her new friend's intentions for her, and Linda implores an increasingly guilty Arthur to give up the criminal life(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Pubs, Ponds and Power: The Story of the Village
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson explores the hidden villages of London. Despite many rural settlements like Hornsey and Dagenham being swallowed up by the expansion of London, Hampstead residents successfully fought to preserve their village heritage, while in recent years Londoners have created a new breed of urban villages like Crouch End and Walthamstow(n)

programme.title: A Slow Odyssey
programme.desc: Cameras follow a white tailed eagle around the Isle of Mull on Scotland's west coast. She is the biggest bird around, but that does not stop her being challenged by ravens(n)

programme.title: The Troubles: A Secret History
programme.desc: Reporter Darragh MacIntyre hears the inside story of the peace process from within the IRA and the British government. He discovers new information about the internal battle for control of the IRA during the most delicate days of the peace process and learns about the scale of secrecy that still surrounds the conflict(n)

programme.title: Contact
programme.desc: Director Alan Clarke's 1985 documentary-style drama based on the memoirs of AFN Clarke, an ex-paratrooper who served in Northern Ireland. A platoon of paratroopers on border patrol in South Armagh, conducting stop-and-search duties and exchanging fire with unseen assailants, experience a number of eerie encounters. programme.desc: Part two of two. The comedian continues his journey retracing the footsteps of Victorian naturalist Alfred Russel Wallace by heading to Indonesian archipelago the Moluccas, formerly known by the nickname of the Spice Islands. There Bill encounters boggle-eyed tarsiers, monkeys with Mohican hairstyles and spectacular birds of paradise on his mission to understand how Wallace devised the theory of evolution independently of Charles Darwin(n)

programme.desc: What's it like to sniff, cough, jerk, roll your eyes and swear - and not be able to stop yourself? programme.desc: In this programme we visit three children who are all having a fascinating day out. In Russia, seven-year-old Liza picks blueberries with her grandma - and helps turn them into delicious pancakes. In Ghana, Tyra, who's five, goes to the market with her mum and learns how to do the shopping. And in Jordan, Hamza, who's eight, has a day out in town with his dad, and buys a lovely present for his baby sister, Mimi. We see that although children live in different countries, may look different and speak different languages, the joys and discoveries of childhood are universal.(n)

programme.desc: Small is bored, and with Tiny, Major and Mother all too busy, he asks Granny to play with him. He is doubtful - but he couldn't have been more mistaken as she takes him on an adventure to a place he has never seen before(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Crystal Palace v Manchester City at Selhurst Park and Chelsea v Newcastle United at Stamford Bridge. The Eagles had a woeful recent record against the champions, having lost eight of their previous 10 meetings in all competitions. However, Roy Hodgson's men did pull off a shock away victory over the Citizens in December, winning 3-2 at the Etihad Stadium. Plus, action from Aston Villa v Brighton & Hove Albion at Villa Park, Bournemouth v Norwich City at the Vitality Stadium, Everton v West Ham United at Goodison Park, Leicester City v Burnley at the King Power Stadium, Tottenham Hotspur v Watford at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium, and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Southampton at Molineux(n)

programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps an active couple return to their roots in Shropshire, looking for a property within their £350,000 budget that gives them the great outdoors on their doorstep(n)

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway learns about the mystery of the ringing stones in the Preseli Hills, Bluestone country, before visiting one of Gower's best-kept secrets - a hidden bay close to Swansea(n)

programme.title: X-Ray
programme.desc: Omar Hamdi, Lucy Owen and Rachel Treadaway-Williams present an investigation into the private parking industry, and the people who are handing out parking tickets. There is also an interview with the victim of a beauty craze that left her scarred, and a look at the illegal skin cream for sale in Wales(n)

programme.title: Gareth Edwards' Great Welsh Adventure
programme.desc: Though aged in his seventies, the rugby legend is busier than ever, so wife Maureen persuades him to take life a little easier by exploring the four Welsh canals. The duo take charge of a narrowboat on the Llangollen Canal, but Gareth's stress levels go soon through the roof as he tries to avoid hitting obstacles. Somehow, Wales' much-loved couple still find time to appreciate the breath-taking scenery(n)

programme.title: Richard and Jaco Take on the World
programme.desc: A personal exploration of autism with actor Richard Mylan and his autistic son Jaco. The pair journey from Wales to America and back again, exploring what it means to be autistic and finding out how places from a local barbershop to a national stadium can become more inclusive for autistic people. They visit Sesame Place theme park in Pennsylvania, where Jaco has a chance to meet Julia, Sesame Street's first character with autism(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic gold medallists in cycling and boxing respectively Joanna Rowsell Shand and Luke Campbell, former Great Britain sprinter John Regis and Scotland rugby union star Tommy Seymour join team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In the Forest of Dean, Matt Baker goes on the hunt for the elusive adder, and finds out about a project in the forest that is teaching locals heritage crafts. Margherita Taylor meets the brass band making a 'big noise' in the area. She also explores the extraordinary Puzzlewood to discover how a landscape like this is protected. Adam Henson is on his farm keeping everything crossed as Lexie, his Suffolk Punch mare, gets a pregnancy scan(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman introduce a performance by pop star Mabel and a spectacular group routine by the professionals, before the results are announced and another couple leave the competition for good(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Battle Abbey in East Sussex, where treasures include a diamond ring that was purchased for £1, a 17th-century violin and a unique telegram from Adolf Hitler. A set of iconic weather signs and a valuable princess's dress are among the other items turning up on the day(n)

programme.title: World on Fire
programme.desc: Harry and his unit are fighting for their lives in the town of Louvain, Belgium, where the German forces far outnumber those of the Allies. Grzegorz and Konrad are woken by a German troop, while back in Warsaw Kasia and Tomasz's resistance activity has horrific consequences. Webster is shocked to learn more about the rise of anti-Semitism in Paris when a Jewish couple come seeking his help(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Manchester United v Liverpool. Mark Chapman presents action from the day's only Premier League fixture, as the fierce rivals met at Old Trafford. Liverpool had not won here since the 2013/14, but they will have gone into the match with confidence after winning their first eight league games this season, while United had endured a disappointing start to the campaign(n)

programme.title: Football Going Vegan: Jermaine Jenas Investigates
programme.desc: The former player explores the rise of veganism in football, a somewhat controversial subject. He talks to celebrity nutritionist Rhiannon Lambert, cooks a couple of dishes with Instagram chef Miguel Barclay, while also spending the day at the first ever vegan football club, Forest Green Rovers. Along his journey, Jermaine sees if he can incorporate a vegan lifestyle into his family life and goes head to head with vegan activist Ed Winters, as well as catching up with vegan footballer Chris Smalling(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes is disappointed to discover she is not invited to Maria's hen party because of her age, and decides to gatecrash the event, only to end up at the wrong venue. She then tries to impress the bride-to-be's mother during a dinner date, but is so nervous she turns to drink, and the evening descends into chaos. Comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: He was the rock star of investing. Hundreds of thousands of people trusted him with their savings and pensions. But Neil Woodford's funds lost billions and now investors are waiting to find out how much of their money they will get back. So how can you know who to trust with your cash? Richard Bilton investigates the secretive world of fund managers - the people who gamble with your money(n)

programme.title: Dublin Murders
programme.desc: As Katy is finally laid to rest, Rob continues to be haunted by memories of the past and Jonathan Devlin and his friends' disturbing presence in the woods on the fateful day Peter and Jamie disappeared. The public and media pressure to find Katy's killer is intense, and Superintendent O'Kelly is concerned that all lines of inquiry have led to naught. Rob, however, is increasingly certain that his memories are leading him to the truth. Cassie receives a frantic dawn phone call from Frank, who is in Wicklow following the trail of corrupt land deals along the path of the planned motorway(n)

programme.title: The Incurable Optimist
programme.desc: Documentary about a woman who has a life-threatening genetic condition. The programme explores how she lives under the constant threat of death, a situation which has given her a uniquely positive perspective on life(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Mairead's much-loved fridge finally gives up on them. Remembering the item was originally bought in a town many miles away, Mairead decides that a family trip to that same place to buy a replacement will be just the thing to bond with her son. After some discussion Jock is eventually included, bringing along his keen navigation skills and unique sense of entertainment to pass the time. During the journey, all the travellers learn lessons about family connections. Comedy, starring Alex Murphy and Chris Walley(n)

programme.desc: Frank comes up with a plan for Cassie to go undercover as her double to discover who killed her, but she refuses to be involved. programme.desc: When Jesy Nelson rose to fame with pop band Little Mix, she was abused online for being 'the fat one'. In this personal documentary, the singer opens up about the impact cyberbullies had on her life and the effect on her mental health. Her fellow Little Mix members Perrie, Leigh-Anne and Jade discuss how Jesy's torment affected the ban, while her boyfriend, Love Island star Chris Hughes, recalls falling in love with someone who was clearly damaged by the barrage of negativity she experienced from social media trolls(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: He was the rock star of investing. Hundreds of thousands of people trusted him with their savings and pensions. But Neil Woodford's funds lost billions and now investors are waiting to find out how much of their money they will get back. So how can you know who to trust with your cash? Richard Bilton investigates the secretive world of fund managers - the people who gamble with your money(n)

programme.title: Dublin Murders
programme.desc: As Katy is finally laid to rest, Rob continues to be haunted by memories of the past and Jonathan Devlin and his friends' disturbing presence in the woods on the fateful day Peter and Jamie disappeared. The public and media pressure to find Katy's killer is intense, and Superintendent O'Kelly is concerned that all lines of inquiry have led to naught. Rob, however, is increasingly certain that his memories are leading him to the truth. Cassie receives a frantic dawn phone call from Frank, who is in Wicklow following the trail of corrupt land deals along the path of the planned motorway(n)

programme.title: The Met: Policing London
programme.desc: The observational documentary about the Metropolitan Police continues with the ongoing problem of moped crime. A young woman is rushed to hospital after being violently mugged by two moped-riding robbers who took her phone and handbag. The detectives get a lead just 15 minutes later when two men use the victim's bank card. But tracking them proves more difficult. The film also follows the Met officers keeping crowds safe at the Notting Hill Carnival, at a time when knife crime in the capital has become a major concern(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Mairead's much-loved fridge finally gives up on them. Remembering the item was originally bought in a town many miles away, Mairead decides that a family trip to that same place to buy a replacement will be just the thing to bond with her son. After some discussion Jock is eventually included, bringing along his keen navigation skills and unique sense of entertainment to pass the time. During the journey, all the travellers learn lessons about family connections. Comedy, starring Alex Murphy and Chris Walley(n)

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, as guests including comedian Ross Noble joining regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Original Terminator stars Arnold Schwarzenegger and Linda Hamilton join the host to discuss their being reunited in the movie's latest instalment, Terminator: Dark Fate. Plus, iconic lead singer of Blondie, Debbie Harry talks about her autobiography, Face It, and Nadiya Hussain discusses her frank and honest new memoir, Finding My Voice. programme.title: EastEnders

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Stevie is torn between his mother and sister as he tries to hold the family together, while Lenny's plan to lie low in prison is under threat, and Alex ups his romantic game to convince Nicole he will always be there for her(n)

programme.title: Dublin Murders
programme.desc: Frank comes up with a plan for Cassie to go undercover as her double to discover who killed her, but she refuses to be involved. However, a savage rejection from Rob that leaves her feeling betrayed leads her to have a change of heart. As the investigation into Katy drags to a halt, Rob becomes fixated on tracking down two of Jonathan's old friends, convinced they are the key to solving both mysteries(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric agrees to undergo the dangerous brain surgery proposed by neurosurgeon Max. During the procedure, Ric has visions of his dead son and lost love, who present him with a terrible choice between life and death. A pregnant Helen returns to Holby, and Dom tries to support Lofty as he faces the prospect of fatherhood(n)

programme.title: Jesy Nelson: 'Odd One Out'
programme.desc: When Jesy Nelson rose to fame with pop band Little Mix, she was abused online for being 'the fat one'. In this personal documentary, the singer opens up about the impact cyberbullies had on her life and the effect on her mental health. Her fellow Little Mix members Perrie, Leigh-Anne and Jade discuss how Jesy's torment affected the ban, while her boyfriend, Love Island star Chris Hughes, recalls falling in love with someone who was clearly damaged by the barrage of negativity she experienced from social media trolls(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Crystal Palace v Manchester City at Selhurst Park and Chelsea v Newcastle United at Stamford Bridge. The Eagles had a woeful recent record against the champions, having lost eight of their previous 10 meetings in all competitions. However, Roy Hodgson's men did pull off a shock away victory over the Citizens in December, winning 3-2 at the Etihad Stadium. Plus, action from Aston Villa v Brighton & Hove Albion at Villa Park, Bournemouth v Norwich City at the Vitality Stadium, Everton v West Ham United at Goodison Park, Leicester City v Burnley at the King Power Stadium, Tottenham Hotspur v Watford at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium, and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Southampton at Molineux(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy-makers on the key issues(n)

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Topical debate covering religious, moral and ethical issues, with guest panellists and contributions from viewers. Presented by Sean Fletcher and Ria Hebden(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Doctors Jo and Angus Goodson trial a week living in Tauranga, to test out Jo's hunch that a life in New Zealand will serve them better. Presented by Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Thomas Plant hosts as two soldiers go up against a pair of brothers, with the teams joined by experts Nick Hall and Natasha Raskin Sharp to scour the Norfolk Antiques Fair to buy three items they hope will make a profit at auction(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley discovers the secrets of sacred geometry at the restored Gorton Monastery in Manchester and meets Christians sharing the love of God in the community(n)

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes(n)

programme.title: Community Life
programme.desc: Habitat for Humanity appeals for new volunteers, and the programme takes a train journey with the team at Whitehead's Railway Museum(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is on a property search in rural Somerset, helping a couple invest their £500,000 in a home that strikes the balance between country charm and practical open planning(n)

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin meet the Waltons in Nottinghamshire, a family with a high-sugar diet that is having a serious impact on their health and their bank balance. Couple Rita and Pete have both been diagnosed with Type 2 Diabetes, and Gregg and Chris are stunned by this couple's serious sugar addiction when they oversee their shopping(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore enlists the help of Mark Haig to turn a Second World War field telephone into something functional again. She also brings an Edwardian single bed into the 21st century(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Contestants include Lilah Parsons, Toby Anstis, Alexandra Fletcher, Nick Pickard, Lisa Maffia, Mutya Buena, Guy Henry and Paul Bradley(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In the Forest of Dean, Matt Baker goes on the hunt for the elusive adder, and finds out about a project in the forest that is teaching locals heritage crafts. Margherita Taylor meets the brass band making a 'big noise' in the area. She also explores the extraordinary Puzzlewood to discover how a landscape like this is protected. Adam Henson is on his farm keeping everything crossed as Lexie, his Suffolk Punch mare, gets a pregnancy scan(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman introduce a performance by pop star Mabel and a spectacular group routine by the professionals, before the results are announced and another couple leave the competition for good(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Battle Abbey in East Sussex, where treasures include a diamond ring that was purchased for £1, a 17th-century violin and a unique telegram from Adolf Hitler. A set of iconic weather signs and a valuable princess's dress are among the other items turning up on the day(n)

programme.title: World on Fire
programme.desc: Harry and his unit are fighting for their lives in the town of Louvain, Belgium, where the German forces far outnumber those of the Allies. Grzegorz and Konrad are woken by a German troop, while back in Warsaw Kasia and Tomasz's resistance activity has horrific consequences. Webster is shocked to learn more about the rise of anti-Semitism in Paris when a Jewish couple come seeking his help(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Manchester United v Liverpool. Mark Chapman presents action from the day's only Premier League fixture, as the fierce rivals met at Old Trafford. Liverpool had not won here since the 2013/14, but they will have gone into the match with confidence after winning their first eight league games this season, while United had endured a disappointing start to the campaign(n)

programme.title: Football Going Vegan: Jermaine Jenas Investigates
programme.desc: The former player explores the rise of veganism in football, a somewhat controversial subject. He talks to celebrity nutritionist Rhiannon Lambert, cooks a couple of dishes with Instagram chef Miguel Barclay, while also spending the day at the first ever vegan football club, Forest Green Rovers. Along his journey, Jermaine sees if he can incorporate a vegan lifestyle into his family life and goes head to head with vegan activist Ed Winters, as well as catching up with vegan footballer Chris Smalling(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic gold medallists in cycling and boxing respectively Joanna Rowsell Shand and Luke Campbell, former Great Britain sprinter John Regis and Scotland rugby union star Tommy Seymour join team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Pop Goes Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Music and news footage from 1983, the year in which the NI Assembly continued to sit but remained without any powers due to a Nationalist boycott. programme.title: RWC 2019 Live
programme.desc: Wales v France (Kick-off 8.15am). Coverage of the third last-eight fixture, which takes place at Oita Stadium in Oita Prefecture. Wales topped Pool D with four wins from four, including an impressive victory over Australia, but were not at their fluent best in their last two matches against Fiji and Uruguay. Meanwhile, France finished second in Pool C behind England after the scheduled meeting between the sides was cancelled due to Typhoon Hagibis(n)

programme.title: RWC 2019 Live
programme.desc: Japan v South Africa (Kick-off 11.15am). All the action from the final last-eight match at Tokyo Stadium. The Springboks finished second behind New Zealand in Pool B and are now looking to avenge a shock defeat at the hands of Japan in the pool stage of the 2015 World Cup. However, the hosts have demonstrated that win was no fluke by defeating the likes of Ireland and Scotland to top Pool A(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh heads to Swindon to surprise Lorraine, a retired RAF servicewoman and devoted charity volunteer. Because of a spinal injury, Lorraine struggles with mobility issues and lives with her full-time support team - Leonbergers Poppy and Doris. Poppy is a retired therapy dog and Doris is Lorraine's assistance dog, and helps with chores such as laundry and fetching household items. The team creates a fabulous low-maintenance garden as a calming retreat for both Lorraine and her canine family(n)

programme.title: Free Willy 3: The Rescue (1997)
programme.desc: The amiable killer whale is reunited with his human friend Jesse, now a teenager working on a research ship. Jesse meets a boy whose father is secretly involved in poaching whales for their meat, and persuades the youngster to help him protect Willy and his pod from harm. Family adventure, starring Jason James Richter, August Schellenberg and Annie Corley(n)

programme.title: Harry Potter and the Prisoner of Azkaban (2004)
programme.desc: The teenage wizard is alarmed to learn a dangerous fugitive sorcerer is searching for him. Meanwhile, a new teacher at Hogwarts helps the bespectacled youngster to protect himself from the school's soul-sucking security guards. The third in the fantasy adventure series, starring Daniel Radcliffe, Emma Watson, Rupert Grint, Gary Oldman, David Thewlis, Michael Gambon, Robbie Coltrane and Alan Rickman(n)

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the big-prize quiz in which another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with the lifelines Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend, 50-50 and Ask the Host available to them if they get stuck(n)

programme.title: Harry & Meghan: An African Journey
programme.desc: When the Duke and Duchess of Sussex took baby son Archie on their recent tour of southern Africa, News at Ten anchor Tom Bradby was with them throughout. Here Bradby gains intimate access to the royal couple, speaking to them about the causes and issues they care most about and exploring some of the pressures and challenges they face as a young family in the glare of the world's media(n)

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson returns to conclude the earlier show. So far this series nobody has won the million - and only five contestants have picked up the top prize since the programme began. Will a sixth lucky player be walking away with the jackpot this evening?(n)

programme.title: Peston
programme.desc: Political magazine show presented by Robert Peston, featuring major interviews with members of Westminster, topical guests and cultural figures. With updates from social media taking in perspectives from viewers, experts and key-players throughout the programme(n)

programme.title: Inside Prison: Britain Behind Bars
programme.desc: The reality of life inside one Britain's 30 Young Offenders Institutions - HMYOI Deerbolt in County Durham. Following weeks of violence and unrest, caused by the influx of drugs and mobile phone being smuggled in, a clampdown has been put in force. Acting on intelligence that inmates have been targeting the weekly Muslim prayer service to exchange illicit items, officers, along with the Imam Muhammad Meah, face a battle to regain control. Elsewhere at HMP Bullingdon, officers in the Segregation Unit are dealing with a prisoner on a Dirty Protest who refuses to leave the wing and attempt to appease the situation with newly recruited prison officer Beth Buchanan(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase

programme.title: Coronation Street Icons: Audrey Roberts - 40 Fabulous Years
programme.desc: A celebration of Sue Nicholls' time on the famous cobbles, which began in April 1979. The actress relives some of Audrey's quirky highlights and most dramatic lows, while Tracy Shaw and Nikki Sanderson - who played salon girls Maxine and Candice - return to the Street to surprise Sue. Brian Capron also reminisces about their dramatic scenes together during the storyline involving his serial killer character Richard Hillman. Narrated by Sally Lindsay(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: David insists on punishing Josh himself, Daniel seeks solace in Bethany's arms, and Max makes a sincere apology to a relieved Shona(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell present a special edition of the series, dedicated to the worldwide search for the remains of soldiers killed during the First World War with no known graves. The programme follows the work of an elite all-female team at the Ministry of Defence known as the 'War Detectives', whose job is to locate the final resting places of 500,000 service personnel who lost their lives during the conflict(n)

programme.title: Harry & Meghan: An African Journey
programme.desc: When the Duke and Duchess of Sussex took baby son Archie on their recent tour of southern Africa, News at Ten anchor Tom Bradby was with them throughout. programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: The team tackles one of their most difficult plots in a garden in south Wales, helping father-of-three Chris Garlick, who lost his hands and feet to sepsis just 22 months earlier. Katie Rushworth creates a contemporary style water-feature-cum bench that would look good in any garden and Frances Tophill shows how soft naturalistic planting with the emphasis on scent can create the perfect garden for wellness(n)

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Japan
programme.desc: The actress heads to the island of Shikoku hoping to gain a better understanding of Japanese Buddhism. She then takes a bullet train to another island - Kyushu - where she finds the Henn Na Hotel, the world's first robot hotel. At Nagasaki, she visits Shiroyama Elementary school, one of the only buildings to survive the atomic bomb dropped on the city in 1945. Joanna then travels to Sakurajima, one of the country's most active volcanoes, before heading to the islands of Okinawa where one of the bloodiest battles of the Second World War was fought(n)

programme.title: RWC 2019 Live
programme.desc: Wales v France (Kick-off 8.15am). Coverage of the third last-eight fixture, which takes place at Oita Stadium in Oita Prefecture. Wales topped Pool D with four wins from four, including an impressive victory over Australia, but were not at their fluent best in their last two matches against Fiji and Uruguay. Meanwhile, France finished second in Pool C behind England after the scheduled meeting between the sides was cancelled due to Typhoon Hagibis(n)

programme.title: RWC 2019 Live
programme.desc: Japan v South Africa (Kick-off 11.15am). All the action from the final last-eight match at Tokyo Stadium. The Springboks finished second behind New Zealand in Pool B and are now looking to avenge a shock defeat at the hands of Japan in the pool stage of the 2015 World Cup. However, the hosts have demonstrated that win was no fluke by defeating the likes of Ireland and Scotland to top Pool A(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh heads to Swindon to surprise Lorraine, a retired RAF servicewoman and devoted charity volunteer. Because of a spinal injury, Lorraine struggles with mobility issues and lives with her full-time support team - Leonbergers Poppy and Doris. Poppy is a retired therapy dog and Doris is Lorraine's assistance dog, and helps with chores such as laundry and fetching household items. The team creates a fabulous low-maintenance garden as a calming retreat for both Lorraine and her canine family(n)

programme.title: Free Willy 3: The Rescue (1997)
programme.desc: The amiable killer whale is reunited with his human friend Jesse, now a teenager working on a research ship. Jesse meets a boy whose father is secretly involved in poaching whales for their meat, and persuades the youngster to help him protect Willy and his pod from harm. Family adventure, starring Jason James Richter, August Schellenberg and Annie Corley(n)

programme.title: Harry Potter and the Prisoner of Azkaban (2004)
programme.desc: The teenage wizard is alarmed to learn a dangerous fugitive sorcerer is searching for him. Meanwhile, a new teacher at Hogwarts helps the bespectacled youngster to protect himself from the school's soul-sucking security guards. programme.title: Inside Prison: Britain Behind Bars
programme.desc: The reality of life inside one Britain's 30 Young Offenders Institutions - HMYOI Deerbolt in County Durham. Following weeks of violence and unrest, caused by the influx of drugs and mobile phone being smuggled in, a clampdown has been put in force. Acting on intelligence that inmates have been targeting the weekly Muslim prayer service to exchange illicit items, officers, along with the Imam Muhammad Meah, face a battle to regain control. Elsewhere at HMP Bullingdon, officers in the Segregation Unit are dealing with a prisoner on a Dirty Protest who refuses to leave the wing and attempt to appease the situation with newly recruited prison officer Beth Buchanan(n)
